  "findings": [
    "Hot work permit scope exceeded — work performed outside the authorized area or conditions defined in the permit."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop all hot work immediately.",
    "Notify the permit-authorizing individual (PAI) and site safety supervisor.",
    "Re-evaluate the work area and tasks to issue a new or revised hot work permit.",
    "Re-inspect the area for fire hazards before resuming work."
  ],
  "rationale": "Per NFPA 51B and OSHA 1926 Subpart J, hot work must be conducted strictly within the scope of the issued permit. Exceeding the permit scope invalidates the required fire prevention and safety precautions, creating an immediate fire and life-safety hazard. The work is non-compliant and must be halted until a proper permit is obtained.",
  "references": [
    "NFPA 51B: Chapter 5 (Permit Authorizing Individual)",
    "NFPA 51B: Section 5.6 (Permit Issuance and Scope)",
    "OSHA 1926.352 (Fire prevention)"
  ]
}
